title: Ambu King Vision aBlade Video Laryngoscope - Display Does Not Power On
description: Troubleshooting a King Vision aBlade display that will not power on due to battery, contact, accessory, or external damage issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the device is not needed for an active airway procedure before troubleshooting. If the King Vision display fails during setup or patient care, provide clinical staff with another ready video laryngoscope or direct laryngoscope per facility procedure. Expected outcome: Patient care is not delayed by a nonfunctional display.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Power Failure
steps.instructions: Press the power button and observe the display. Check whether the unit is completely blank, briefly flashes, shows a low battery indication, or powers off immediately. Expected outcome: The failure mode is confirmed and documented.

steps.title: Check Battery Installation
steps.instructions: Open the battery compartment and verify the batteries are installed in the correct polarity. Remove and reinstall the batteries, ensuring they seat firmly against the contacts. Expected outcome: Display powers on normally if the issue was caused by loose or incorrectly installed batteries. If resolved, stop troubleshooting.

steps.title: Replace with Known-Good Batteries
steps.instructions: Install fresh, facility-approved batteries. Do not rely on batteries removed from another unknown device. Expected outcome: Display powers on if the original batteries were depleted, weak, or expired. If resolved, stop troubleshooting.

steps.title: Inspect Battery Contacts
steps.instructions: Check the battery contacts for corrosion, bent terminals, fluid residue, or visible contamination. Do not scrape aggressively or use excessive force. Clean only according to facility-approved procedure. Expected outcome: Contacts appear clean, intact, and able to maintain battery pressure.

steps.title: Inspect the Battery Door and Housing
steps.instructions: Verify the battery door closes securely and is not cracked, loose, or missing. Inspect the display housing for impact damage, liquid intrusion, damaged seams, or evidence of being dropped. Expected outcome: Display housing and battery compartment are physically intact.

steps.title: Remove the Blade and Retest the Display
steps.instructions: Disconnect the aBlade and attempt to power on the display by itself. A damaged or improperly seated blade should not be allowed to confuse the initial power check. Expected outcome: Display powers on without the blade if the attached blade was contributing to the issue. If resolved, replace the blade and retest.

steps.title: Test with a Known-Good aBlade
steps.instructions: Attach a known-good compatible aBlade and power the unit on again. Confirm the blade locks in properly and does not place stress on the display connection. Expected outcome: Display powers on and produces an image if the issue was related to the blade or blade connection. If resolved, remove the defective blade from use.

steps.title: Check for Intermittent Power
steps.instructions: With fresh batteries installed, gently move the display in normal handling positions. Do not twist, flex, or stress the housing. Watch for flickering, rebooting, or power loss. Expected outcome: Display remains powered without interruption. Intermittent behavior suggests battery-contact, power-button, or internal connection failure.

steps.title: Confirm Cleaning or Fluid Exposure History
steps.instructions: Ask whether the device was recently cleaned, soaked, sprayed heavily, dropped, or stored with batteries installed. Excess liquid or residue near the battery compartment can prevent reliable power-on. Expected outcome: Any likely external exposure is identified and documented.

steps.title: Do Not Attempt Internal Repair During Floor Troubleshooting
steps.instructions: If the display remains blank after fresh batteries, battery contact inspection, blade removal, and known-good accessory testing, stop troubleshooting. Expected outcome: The device is removed from clinical use rather than being repeatedly tested in patient-care areas.

CCR.Complaint: Clinical staff reported the Ambu King Vision aBlade display would not power on during airway setup.
CCR.Cause: Fresh batteries were installed and the battery contacts, battery door, housing, and blade connection were checked, but the display remained blank.
CCR.Resolution: Device was removed from service, labeled Out of Service, and sent for bench evaluation due to suspected internal display or power-control failure.

helpfulDetails: Display blank, flickering, or briefly powering on
Any drop, cleaning, fluid exposure, or corrosion noted

title: Ambu King Vision aBlade Video Laryngoscope - Low Battery / Flashing Battery Indicator
description: Troubleshooting low battery warnings, flashing battery indicators, weak display power, or intermittent shutdown during King Vision aBlade use.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions:
Confirm the King Vision aBlade is not being relied on for an active airway attempt before troubleshooting.

If the device is being used during a procedure and the battery indicator is flashing or the display becomes unstable, clinical staff should switch to another ready airway device immediately.

Expected outcome: Patient care is moved to a dependable device before battery-related troubleshooting continues.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Battery Indicator
steps.instructions:
Power on the display and observe the battery icon or flashing battery indicator.

Confirm whether the issue is:

Flashing battery icon
Low battery message or symbol
Display powers on briefly then shuts off
Display dims or flickers
Unit fails to power on after a low battery warning

Expected outcome: The battery-related symptom is confirmed and documented.

steps.title: Replace the Batteries With New Approved Batteries
steps.instructions:
Remove the battery compartment cover and replace the batteries with new, correct-type batteries as specified for the device.

Do not reuse partially depleted batteries from another device.

Expected outcome: The display powers on normally and the low battery indicator clears.

If this resolves the issue, return the device to service after functional verification and stop troubleshooting.

steps.title: Check Battery Orientation
steps.instructions:
Verify each battery is installed in the correct polarity direction.

Incorrect battery orientation may allow no power, intermittent power, or a continued low battery indication.

Expected outcome: Batteries are seated correctly and the display powers on without a flashing battery warning.

steps.title: Inspect the Battery Compartment
steps.instructions:
Remove the batteries and inspect the battery compartment for:

Corrosion
Bent or recessed contacts
Fluid residue
Loose springs
Cracked plastic
Evidence of cleaning solution intrusion

Expected outcome: Battery contacts are clean, dry, intact, and making proper contact.

If corrosion, fluid intrusion, or damaged contacts are found, remove the device from service.

steps.title: Verify the Battery Cover Secures Properly
steps.instructions:
Reinstall the battery cover and confirm it closes fully without looseness or movement.

A loose cover can allow momentary battery contact loss during handling.

Expected outcome: Battery compartment remains secure during normal handling.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Device
steps.instructions:
Turn the display off, wait briefly, and power it back on after installing fresh batteries.

Observe whether the low battery indicator returns immediately.

Expected outcome: The unit starts normally and the battery indicator remains stable.

steps.title: Check for Intermittent Power During Gentle Handling
steps.instructions:
With fresh batteries installed, gently move the display as it would be handled during setup.

Do not drop, strike, or stress the device.

Watch for flickering, shutdown, or battery indicator changes.

Expected outcome: Display remains powered and stable during normal handling.

If the display flickers or shuts off, suspect battery contact damage or an internal power issue.

steps.title: Inspect the Display and Blade Connection Area
steps.instructions:
Confirm the aBlade connection area is clean, dry, and free of visible contamination.

A connection issue is not usually the primary cause of a low battery indicator, but poor connection or moisture can create confusing power or display behavior.

Expected outcome: Device connection points appear clean and undamaged.

steps.title: Perform a Basic Functional Check
steps.instructions:
Attach a known-good compatible aBlade and power on the device.

Confirm:

Display turns on normally
Image appears
Battery indicator is stable
No flickering occurs
Device remains powered during normal movement

Expected outcome: The device passes a practical operational check before being returned to service.

CCR.Complaint: Clinical staff reported the Ambu King Vision aBlade display showed a flashing low battery indicator during airway setup.
CCR.Cause: Batteries were depleted, and the low battery indicator cleared after new batteries were installed with correct polarity.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced batteries, inspected battery contacts and cover, verified stable power and video image with a compatible aBlade, and returned the device to service.

title: Ambu King Vision aBlade Video Laryngoscope - No image after blade attachment
description: Troubleshooting no video image after attaching a King Vision aBlade due to blade seating, contacts, battery, display, or accessory issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: If the device fails during airway preparation or active airway management, clinical staff should immediately use another ready video laryngoscope or alternate airway device. Do not troubleshoot the Ambu King Vision aBlade on an active patient. Expected outcome: Patient care is transferred to a reliable airway visualization device before troubleshooting continues.

steps.title: Confirm the Reported Failure
steps.instructions: Power on the display and attach the aBlade while observing the screen. Confirm whether the issue is: Completely black screen; Display powers on but shows no camera image; Image appears briefly, then drops out; Error or abnormal display behavior after blade attachment. Expected outcome: The failure mode is confirmed and documented.

steps.title: Verify the Display Powers On Normally
steps.instructions: Turn the King Vision display on without the blade attached. Check for normal startup behavior, screen illumination, and battery indication if available. Expected outcome: The display powers on normally before evaluating the blade connection. If the display does not power on, stop this guide and troubleshoot the display power or battery condition first.

steps.title: Check the Battery Condition
steps.instructions: Replace the batteries with known-good, properly installed batteries if the display is dim, intermittent, or fails during blade attachment. Confirm the battery contacts are clean and that the battery compartment is fully closed. Expected outcome: The display remains powered on and stable during blade attachment. If replacing the batteries restores video, return the unit to service after functional verification.

steps.title: Remove and Reattach the aBlade
steps.instructions: Remove the aBlade and inspect how it seats onto the display. Reattach it firmly and evenly, making sure it is fully engaged and not angled, partially inserted, or loose. Expected outcome: The display detects the blade and produces a live camera image. If reseating the blade restores the image, verify stable operation with gentle handling and stop.

steps.title: Inspect the Blade-to-Display Connection Area
steps.instructions: Look at the blade interface and display connection area for: Debris; Fluid residue; Bent or damaged contacts; Cracked plastic; Worn latch area; Signs of cleaning-fluid intrusion. Do not scrape contacts aggressively or use excessive liquid. Expected outcome: Connection surfaces are clean, dry, and physically intact.

steps.title: Try a Known-Good aBlade
steps.instructions: Attach a new or known-good compatible Ambu King Vision aBlade. Expected outcome: The display shows a clear image with the known-good blade. If the known-good blade works, remove the original blade from use and document the disposable blade failure.

steps.title: Check for Blade Damage or Packaging Issues
steps.instructions: Inspect the suspect aBlade for cracked housing, damaged camera window, loose connection area, or contamination from packaging or storage. Expected outcome: The blade is physically intact and suitable for use. If the blade appears damaged, discard it according to facility policy and verify the display with another blade.

steps.title: Verify the Display with Multiple Blades
steps.instructions: If one blade does not work, test the display with at least one additional known-good blade when available. Expected outcome: A properly functioning display should produce an image with known-good blades. If multiple known-good blades fail to produce an image, suspect the display or blade connector interface.

steps.title: Check for Intermittent Image During Handling
steps.instructions: With a known-good blade attached, gently move the display and blade connection without stressing the device. Watch for flickering, image dropout, or loss of video. Expected outcome: Image remains stable during normal handling. If the image cuts in and out, remove the display from service for repair evaluation.

steps.title: Inspect for External Damage
steps.instructions: Check the King Vision display for signs of impact, cracked housing, loose battery door, damaged screen, or contamination around the blade connection. Expected outcome: No external damage is present that would explain the connection failure. If damage is found, remove the display from service.

steps.title: Perform a Final Functional Check
steps.instructions: Attach a known-good blade and confirm: Display powers on; Blade is recognized; Live image appears; Image remains stable; Camera view is clear; No flicker or dropout occurs during normal handling. Expected outcome: The device is ready for safe clinical use.

CCR.Complaint: Clinical staff reported that the Ambu King Vision aBlade produced no image after blade attachment.
CCR.Cause: Troubleshooting found that the display powered on, but no video image appeared with the reported blade; known-good blade testing confirmed the original blade connection failed.
CCR.Resolution: Defective aBlade was removed from use, display passed functional check with a known-good blade, and unit was returned to service.

helpfulDetails: Whether the display powered on normally
Battery condition or battery replacement performed
Whether the issue occurred with one blade or multiple blades
Blade lot number if a disposable blade issue is suspected
Any flickering or intermittent image behavior
Signs of fluid residue, debris, impact, or cracked housing
Whether a known-good blade restored the image

title: Ambu King Vision aBlade Video Laryngoscope - Blurred or Reversed Video Image
description: Troubleshooting blurred, distorted, upside-down, or reversed video caused by blade seating, lens contamination, display position, or device damage.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: If the King Vision aBlade is being used during an active airway attempt, do not continue troubleshooting while patient care is delayed. Have clinical staff move to an alternate airway device or backup video laryngoscope if the image is not immediately usable. Expected outcome: The patient is not dependent on a laryngoscope with an unreliable image.

steps.title: Confirm the Reported Image Problem
steps.instructions: Power on the display and confirm whether the issue is: Blurry image; Fogged or obscured image; Dark or distorted image; Image appears reversed; Image orientation changes when the handle or display is moved. Expected outcome: The exact video issue is identified before parts are swapped.

steps.title: Check the aBlade for Lens Obstruction
steps.instructions: Inspect the distal camera window on the disposable aBlade. Look for: Protective film left in place; Packaging residue; Fluid, blood, secretions, or cleaning residue; Scratches, cracks, or clouding on the camera window. If contamination is present, replace the disposable aBlade. Do not attempt to reuse a compromised disposable blade. Expected outcome: A clean, undamaged blade produces a clear image. If resolved, stop.

steps.title: Verify the Blade Is Fully Seated
steps.instructions: Remove and reinstall the aBlade onto the display/handle assembly. Confirm it locks into place evenly and does not feel loose, tilted, or partially engaged. Expected outcome: A properly seated blade provides a stable, correctly oriented image. If resolved, stop.

steps.title: Try a Known-Good Disposable aBlade
steps.instructions: Install a new, unopened, compatible King Vision aBlade. This helps separate a blade-related camera issue from a display or handle issue. Expected outcome: If the new blade produces a clear, correctly oriented image, the original blade was defective, contaminated, or damaged. Remove the bad blade from use and stop.

steps.title: Check Display Position and Viewing Angle
steps.instructions: Confirm the image is not being interpreted as reversed because the display is rotated, viewed from an unusual angle, or held in an incorrect orientation. Reposition the display and compare the image to expected camera direction. Expected outcome: The displayed image should match expected blade movement and orientation.

steps.title: Inspect the Display/Handle Connection Area
steps.instructions: Check the connector area where the blade interfaces with the reusable display. Look for: Debris; Bent or damaged contacts; Moisture; Cracked plastic; Loose fit between blade and display. Do not scrape contacts aggressively or disassemble the unit. Expected outcome: The connector area is clean, dry, intact, and allows the blade to seat correctly.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Device
steps.instructions: Turn the display off, wait briefly, and power it back on with a known-good blade installed. Expected outcome: The image initializes normally. If the image returns to normal, document the event and verify repeat operation before returning to service.

steps.title: Check Battery Condition
steps.instructions: If the image is dim, unstable, delayed, or intermittently distorted, verify the battery condition or replace the battery if applicable to the device configuration. Low power can contribute to unreliable display behavior. Expected outcome: The display remains stable with adequate power.

steps.title: Inspect for Physical Damage
steps.instructions: Examine the display, hinge area, housing, screen, and blade interface for evidence of drops, impact, cracking, liquid intrusion, or loose components. Expected outcome: No visible damage is present. If damage is found, remove the unit from service.

steps.title: Perform a Functional Check With Multiple Known-Good Blades
steps.instructions: Test the display with at least one additional known-good compatible blade if available. If blurred or reversed video occurs with multiple blades, the issue is likely with the reusable display/handle assembly. Expected outcome: The device consistently produces a clear, correctly oriented image before return to service.

CCR.Complaint: Clinical staff reported the Ambu King Vision aBlade image was blurry and appeared reversed during pre-use check.
CCR.Cause: Found disposable aBlade camera window contaminated and blade not fully seated on the display assembly.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced the aBlade, reseated the connection, verified clear and correctly oriented video image, and returned the display to service.

helpfulDetails: Whether a new disposable blade was tested
Any signs of fluid intrusion or impact damage
Whether the issue followed the blade or stayed with the display

title: Ambu King Vision aBlade Video Laryngoscope - Blade-to-display connection failure
description: Troubleshooting poor blade connection, no image, split image, frozen image, or display shutdown caused by blade, adapter, or contact issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: If the King Vision aBlade is being prepared for an airway case and the image is not reliable, do not continue with that device. Provide clinical staff with another ready video laryngoscope or alternate airway device before troubleshooting. Expected outcome: Patient care is not delayed by an unreliable display or blade connection.

steps.title: Confirm the Reported Failure
steps.instructions: Ask staff what was observed: No image; Static or snow; Split image; Frozen image; Image drops out when the blade is moved; Display powers off after blade movement; Blade not recognized. Expected outcome: The failure mode is confirmed before parts are swapped. Ambu quick-reference guidance notes that blade connection timing can affect image behavior, including split or frozen image conditions.

steps.title: Check That the Blade Is Connected Before Power-On
steps.instructions: Power the display off. Remove the blade and adapter, then reconnect them fully before powering the display back on. Expected outcome: The display powers up with a live moving image. If the image returns, document improper connection sequence or incomplete blade seating and stop.

steps.title: Inspect the Disposable aBlade
steps.instructions: Check the blade for: Cracked plastic; Bent or damaged connector area; Contamination on the connector; Blood, cleaning residue, or moisture near the connection point; Packaging damage; Wrong blade size or wrong blade type for the adapter. Expected outcome: The blade is clean, intact, and compatible. If damage or contamination is found, discard the disposable blade and retest with a new blade. Ambu describes the King Vision aBlade system as using single-use blades.

steps.title: Inspect the aBlade Adapter
steps.instructions: Remove the aBlade adapter and inspect it externally. Look for: Cracked housing; Loose fit; Bent connector contacts; Debris in the connection channel; Signs of liquid intrusion; Excessive wear from repeated blade insertion. Expected outcome: The adapter seats securely and has no visible damage. If the adapter is damaged, remove it from use and replace or send for evaluation.

steps.title: Reseat the Display, Adapter, and Blade
steps.instructions: Reconnect the components in this order: Display off; Adapter fully seated to display; aBlade fully seated to adapter; Power display on. Gently move the handle and blade while watching the screen. Expected outcome: The image remains stable during light movement. If the image cuts out during movement, suspect a loose adapter, worn connection, or damaged display connection point.

steps.title: Test With a Known-Good Blade
steps.instructions: Use a new or known-good compatible aBlade. Power the display off before connecting the replacement blade. Expected outcome: A stable live image appears. If the issue resolves, the original blade was defective, contaminated, or improperly seated. Discard the failed disposable blade and stop.

steps.title: Test With a Known-Good Adapter
steps.instructions: If available, test the display with a known-good compatible aBlade adapter. Expected outcome: The display recognizes the blade and maintains a stable image. If the issue resolves, replace the original adapter and document adapter failure.

steps.title: Inspect the Display Connection Area
steps.instructions: With the device removed from patient use, inspect the display stem connection area. Look for: Bent pins or contacts; Cracked plastic; Worn connector retention; Moisture or residue; Evidence of cleaning fluid exposure; Loose fit between display and adapter. Expected outcome: The display connection area is clean, dry, and mechanically secure. Do not probe aggressively or attempt internal repair at the bedside.

steps.title: Check for Cleaning or Liquid Exposure
steps.instructions: Ask whether the display was recently cleaned, dropped into fluid, or stored while wet. The King Vision display is not intended to be submerged, and connector openings should be protected from liquid exposure during cleaning. Expected outcome: No liquid contamination is present at the electrical connection. If liquid exposure is suspected, remove the device from service.

steps.title: Verify Battery Condition
steps.instructions: Confirm the display has adequate battery power. Low battery can complicate troubleshooting by causing shutdowns or unstable display behavior. Expected outcome: Battery condition is sufficient for testing. If battery status is low or questionable, replace batteries or use a known-good display according to facility practice, then retest.

steps.title: Perform a Final Functional Check
steps.instructions: With a known-good blade and adapter installed before power-on, confirm: Display powers on; Live image appears; Image does not freeze; Image does not split; Image remains stable with light handling; No visible damage is present; Device is clean and ready for clinical use. Expected outcome: The King Vision aBlade system passes a basic functional check. If the issue is resolved, return the device to service per facility policy.

CCR.Complaint: Clinical staff reported the Ambu King Vision aBlade display showed a frozen image after the blade was connected.
CCR.Cause: Found the disposable aBlade did not maintain a secure connection to the aBlade adapter, while a known-good blade worked normally.
CCR.Resolution: Removed the failed disposable blade from use, verified stable live image with a replacement blade, and returned the display and adapter to service.

helpfulDetails: Whether the blade was connected before power-on
Whether the image was blank, split, frozen, or intermittent
Whether a known-good blade was tested
Whether a known-good adapter was tested
Any visible cracks or loose fit
